stories.title: Texas vs. Texas Tech — WCWS Championship Game 1 Preview (Tonight, 8 PM ET)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
History tonight at the #WCWS.

Texas vs. Texas Tech — first championship rematch since the best-of-three format started in 2005.

Defending champs. No. 11 seed. One title on the line.

8 PM ET on ESPN. 🤠

#HookEm #WreckEm

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
It's game night at Devon Park. 🥎

No. 2 Texas vs. No. 11 Texas Tech for the national title.

Kavan vs. Canady. Power vs. grit. Champion vs. challenger.

Game 1 | Tonight 8 PM ET | ESPN

#WCWS #HookEm #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
The moment softball fans have been waiting for is finally here. 🥎

Tonight at Devon Park in Oklahoma City, No. 2 Texas and No. 11 Texas Tech meet in Game 1 of the 2026 Women's College World Series Championship Series — and this one carries a storyline unlike any final in recent history.

This is the FIRST championship rematch since the WCWS adopted its current best-of-three format back in 2005. One year ago, Texas edged Texas Tech 2-1 to claim the national title. Now the Red Raiders are back — not as an afterthought, but as a legitimate threat with the pitching ace and the scoreboard-tilting power hitter to pull off an upset.

Texas enters at 51-12 as the defending champion and program royalty. Texas Tech comes in at 61-8 after one of the most breathtaking runs in WCWS history, eliminating No. 1 Alabama twice in the same day to earn its spot. Teagan Kavan vs. NiJaree Canady. Power vs. grit. A title defense vs. a revenge tour.

Don't miss it. 8 PM ET on ESPN.

Who are you picking to win it all? Drop your prediction below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Texas vs. Texas Tech. Tonight. 8 PM ET on ESPN. 🤠 The first WCWS championship rematch since 2005. #WCWS #HookEm #WreckEm

stories.title: NiJaree Canady — Historic Pitcher Chases First Title

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
NiJaree Canady is only the 4th pitcher in WCWS history to record a shutout in four different tournaments.

Nancy Evans. Lisa Longaker. Debbie Doom. Canady.

Tonight she goes for the title she hasn't won yet.

#WreckEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech's NiJaree Canady this tournament:

▸ Game 1 vs. Alabama: 2.2 IP, 1 run in relief
▸ Game 2 vs. Alabama: CG shutout, 2 hits, 6 Ks

She's locked in. Can she pitch Texas Tech to its first national title?

#WreckEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
There are great pitchers. And then there is NiJaree Canady.

The Texas Tech ace became only the FOURTH pitcher in Women's College World Series history to record a shutout in four different tournament appearances. She joined an exclusive group: Nancy Evans, Lisa Longaker, and Debbie Doom — names that belong on the Mount Rushmore of college softball pitching greatness.

In Monday's deciding semifinal against No. 1 Alabama, Canady delivered exactly what Texas Tech needed: a complete-game, 2-hit shutout with 6 strikeouts. She held the nation's most decorated offense scoreless. She's done it in four different WCWSs now.

The only thing she doesn't have: a national championship ring.

That changes tonight, or Texas will try to stop it. Canady vs. the defending champions, Game 1, 8 PM ET on ESPN.

Can Canady get the ring she deserves? 🔴⚫
facebook.image_caption: NiJaree Canady: 4th pitcher EVER with WCWS shutouts in four different tournaments. Tonight she goes for the title. 🥎 #WreckEm #WCWS

stories.title: Teagan Kavan — The Reigning MOP Goes for Back-to-Back

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan in Monday's semifinal:

10 Ks. 2 hits. CG shutout vs. Tennessee.

That's your reigning WCWS Most Outstanding Player.

She needs 2 wins to go back-to-back. 🔐

#HookEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
The 2025 WCWS Most Outstanding Player is back on the biggest stage — and she's coming off the most dominant performance of the tournament.

Teagan Kavan threw a complete-game shutout against Tennessee in Monday's semifinal: 10 strikeouts, 2 hits allowed, 0 walks. The final score: 4-0 Texas. It was dominant, efficient, and vintage Kavan.

She now has nine WCWS wins in her career — a Texas program record. She has five career WCWS shutouts, tying Cat Osterman on the all-time program list. She is, by every measure, the ace of the defending champions.

But the question now isn't what Kavan has done. It's what she can do starting tonight, Game 1 against Texas Tech and NiJaree Canady. Two wins. That's all she needs for back-to-back titles and a legacy that will echo in Austin for decades.

Can Kavan go back-to-back? We believe. 🤘
facebook.image_caption: Reigning WCWS MOP Teagan Kavan is ready for her back-to-back bid. 10 Ks. 2 hits. 0 walks. CG shutout vs. Tennessee. She's locked in. 🔐 #HookEm #WCWS

stories.title: Katie Stewart — Power Behind the Longhorns

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Katie Stewart hit her 29th AND 30th HRs of the season vs. Tennessee Monday.

30 HRs = new Texas single-season program record.

The Longhorns have 97 team HRs in 2026 — also a program record.

#HookEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
When Texas needs a big hit, Katie Stewart delivers.

On Monday, the Texas slugger launched her 29th and 30th home runs of the season against Tennessee — blowing past the previous Texas single-season program record of 28 HRs. Just like that, history was made in the middle of a WCWS semifinal sweep.

It didn't end there. Texas as a team now has 97 home runs in 2026, surpassing the 2025 record of 94 — the previous all-time Texas record. The Longhorns hit the ball with the kind of authority that changes games in an instant, and Stewart is the engine of that machine.

She enters tonight's Game 1 with momentum, history on her side, and a clear mission: help bring the title back to Austin. Texas Tech pitching, led by Canady, is the best challenge she'll face all season.

That's what makes tonight so compelling. 🔥
facebook.image_caption: Katie Stewart: 30 HRs, new Texas single-season program record. The Longhorns enter Game 1 tonight as the defending champs — and the most powerful team in college softball. 💪 #HookEm #WCWS

stories.title: Texas Tech's Cinderella Path — No. 11 Seed in the Championship

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Texas Tech's path to the 2026 #WCWS championship:

✅ Beat UCLA 8-7 in extras
✅ Beat No. 1 Alabama (twice, same day)
✅ First No. 11 seed in WCWS Finals

Tonight they play the defending champs for a national title.

#WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
Let's take a moment to appreciate what Texas Tech just did.

The Red Raiders entered the 2026 Women's College World Series as the No. 11 national seed. That means no home games in the early rounds, longer travel, and a path that requires winning six games at the WCWS — the most of any team in the field.

They beat UCLA in a wild 8-7 extra-inning game. Then on Monday, they faced No. 1 Alabama — the top seed in the entire tournament. And they didn't just beat them once. They beat them twice. In the same day.

Mia Williams delivered a walk-off home run — the 26th of her season and the first walk-off of her career — to force the deciding game. Then NiJaree Canady pitched a complete-game shutout to send Texas Tech to the championship series.

Tonight, they play Texas for a national title. It might be the most remarkable run in WCWS history.

Go out and enjoy it. 🤠
facebook.image_caption: No. 11 seed. Beat the No. 1 team twice. Now in the national championship. Texas Tech's 2026 WCWS run is the stuff of legends. 🤠 #WreckEm #WCWS

article.headline: How Texas Tech Became the Most Unlikely Team at the WCWS Championship

## Notes
- Game 1 has NOT been played yet as of this pipeline run (content written pre-game for evening publication)
- All story angles are preview/feature focused
- Story 3/4 are FOLLOW UPs from 06-02 coverage — different angles (player profiles vs. game results)
- "Stewart 31st HR program record" noted in 06-02 pipeline status may have been a miscounting — search results confirm 29th and 30th vs. Tennessee, making 30 the record number confirmed by primary source (TexasLonghorns.com)
